David, after giving instructions about building the temple, establishes the method for the temple service and appoints its officers. When people of the same family worked together, it encouraged them to love and help one another.

Now the people of Israel were so numerous that there should be more employed in the temple service, so that every Israelite who brought an offering might find a Levite ready to assist him. When more work is needed, it is regrettable that there are not more workers. A new heart, a spiritual mind that greatly delights in God’s commandments and can find a refreshing experience in his ordinances, forms the great distinction between the true Christian and all other people in the world. To the spiritual person every service will yield satisfaction. He will always be eager to do the work of the Lord; he is never so happy as when employed for such a good Master, in such a pleasant service. He will not worry whether he is called to take the lead or to oversee others who are placed over him. May we seek and serve the Lord uprightly, and leave all else to his disposal, by faith in his word.
